property.name: dfs.blocksize
property.description: Default block size for new files. Larger block size reduces NameNode memory footprint and improves large file scanning throughput by reducing seek operations.

property.name: dfs.namenode.handler.count
property.description: The number of NameNode RPC server listener threads to process client requests. Rule of thumb: 20 * ln(DataNodes), minimum 10.

property.name: dfs.namenode.service.handler.count
property.description: RPC threads for DataNode to NameNode communication. Isolates write/read reports from client traffic.

property.name: dfs.datanode.max.transfer.threads
property.description: Max concurrent files/sockets open by DataNode. Equivalent to xceivers limit in older versions.

property.name: dfs.datanode.handler.count
property.description: Number of DataNode server threads for client connections.

property.name: dfs.client.read.shortcircuit
property.description: Enables local HDFS client to read blocks directly from local file system without routing through DataNode TCP socket.

property.name: dfs.domain.socket.path
property.description: Unix Domain Socket path used for communications and security handshakes between client and DataNode during short-circuit reads.

property.name: dfs.datanode.balance.bandwidthPerSec
property.description: Maximum amount of bandwidth in bytes per second that each DataNode can use for rebalancing data. High bandwidth accelerates balance operations but might affect client read/write rates.

property.name: dfs.datanode.balance.max.concurrent.moves
property.description: Maximum concurrent thread moves allowed on a DataNode during balance operation.

property.name: dfs.datanode.max.locked.memory
property.description: Memory allocated to cache HDFS blocks in OS RAM. Disabled (0) by default, adjust when using Centralized Cache Management.

property.name: dfs.heartbeat.interval
property.description: Interval in seconds between DataNode heartbeats to NameNode.

property.name: dfs.namenode.heartbeat.recheck-interval
property.description: Interval in milliseconds used to determine when a DataNode is dead.
